claimBoundary: Coverage inventory only. Product gates, engineering preflights, historical exploratory evidence, and untested claims remain distinct.

statusVocabulary.validated-product-gate: Deterministic product or packaging contract; not an Agent comparison.
statusVocabulary.engineering-preflight: Non-formal Agent or fixture evidence outside the frozen result manifest.
statusVocabulary.historical-exploratory: Valid retained evidence from an earlier frozen protocol and product version.
statusVocabulary.blocked: Required external or protocol gate is not complete.

palaceSelfEvaluation.finding: The route stayed inside fixture implementation files and missed both matrices, the machine artifact, all three navigation surfaces, and the direct documentation test.

evolutionRouteSelfEvaluation.finding: The route selected only the v2.2 and v3 plans. It omitted the v1 plan, both result analyses, and the bilingual evolution record needed to answer the task.

studyEvolution.conclusion: No correctness loss was observed, but Full Palace added supported Token, tool-call, and wall-time overhead; useful-memory correctness benefit was not observed.

failedPredecessors.reason: PowerShell interpolated $0.00 in three Palace prompts; comparison invalid.
Windows split writable roots caused patch-verification infrastructure failures; study stopped.

studyEvolution.conclusion: Adaptive reduced Palace-owned payload but did not establish lower end-to-end Token use or wall time than Control; useful-memory fidelity failed in 0.2.1.
Design and release gates exist, but no formal 0.3.0 Agent outcome exists.

coverage.allowedClaim: The local release candidate passes packaging, CLI, MCP, and cross-platform gates.
coverage.forbiddenClaim: The package is already publicly installable.

coverage.allowedClaim: The pinned Zod issue-style task retrieved its known implementation and test twice.
coverage.forbiddenClaim: TypeScript monorepos are generally solved faster or with fewer Tokens.

coverage.allowedClaim: The pinned Requests issue-style task retrieved its known implementation and test twice.
coverage.forbiddenClaim: Python repositories are generally solved faster or with fewer Tokens.

coverage.allowedClaim: A separate small-OSS stratum remains future work.
coverage.forbiddenClaim: Requests retroactively proves a preregistered small-OSS stratum.

coverage.allowedClaim: Two pinned issue-style retrieval tasks passed.
coverage.forbiddenClaim: End-to-end real-repository Agent repair has been validated.

coverage.allowedClaim: This is an explicit evidence gap.
coverage.forbiddenClaim: Memory improves real-project correctness over Control.

coverage.allowedClaim: This is an explicit evidence gap.
coverage.forbiddenClaim: Cross-module architecture routing is validated on real repositories.

coverage.allowedClaim: The package can return a compact bypass contract.
coverage.forbiddenClaim: A compact bypass guarantees the Agent stops exploring.

coverage.allowedClaim: Memory beat structure-only routing in one non-formal fixture run.
All arms rejected stale advice in this synthetic v2.2 block.
v2.2 did not show an end-to-end efficiency advantage over Control; tool-call overhead was supported.
coverage.forbiddenClaim: 0.3.0 has the same effect or is already proven faster.

coverage.blocker: The benchmark dependency and lockfile remain on 0.2.1, the draft plan still pins a pre-release candidate hash, and the clean release gate, freeze commit, and protocol tag are incomplete.
coverage.allowedClaim: The Control-first design is preregistered but unexecuted.
coverage.forbiddenClaim: Any v3 efficiency or correctness outcome exists.

coverage.allowedClaim: npm users can install public 0.3.0 and Codex users can install the version-aligned v0.3.0 plugin from the Git marketplace.
coverage.forbiddenClaim: Successful distribution proves an Agent efficiency or correctness advantage.

nextEvidence: rebind the v3 plan, benchmark dependency, lockfile, and release gate to the immutable public 0.3.0 metadata
freeze and tag the Control-first v3 protocol after a clean exact install
preregister a separate small-OSS size threshold and repository
add real-repository history-dependent and architecture/refactor tasks with hidden objective oracles
